name: Eric Iwersen
title: Transportation and Sustainability Director
hook: Tempe's stated transportation mission centers safer pedestrian and bike movement. The AZ Law Now investigation puts crash data alongside the engineering choices that shape it, and Tempe's record on road redesign is one of the cleaner ones to point to.
personalization_hook: Saw your Tempe Podcast episode on Transportation Plan 2050 and the next-generation vision for Tempe streets.

name: Shelly Seyler
title: Deputy Director, Transportation & Sustainability
hook: Deputy on Tempe's transportation portfolio. Investigation flags road design as the upstream cause, and Tempe's deputy is one of the few municipal voices already on record about pedestrian safety as an engineering issue, not a behavior one.

name: Tara Ford
title: Public Works Director
hook: Public Works owns the actual paving, signal, and crossing decisions in Tempe. Investigation breaks down where AZ road design fails pedestrians and Tara's department is the operational counterpart to Tempe's transportation policy office.
personalization_hook: Saw your Public Works role in Tempe's 2025 water-rate proposal coverage and the city's pavement-quality-index push to get every street above 82 by 2028.

name: Jodie Inman
title: Deputy Public Works Director, Engineering
hook: Engineering deputy. Investigation identifies engineered road choices as the proximate driver of AZ pedestrian deaths, and an engineering deputy reading it sees the lane-width, speed, and crossing geometry callouts directly.
personalization_hook: Saw your Engineering Division leadership at Tempe Public Works covering the city's capital project pipeline.

name: Heather Murphy
title: Public Engagement Coordinator, Street Transportation
hook: Phoenix Streets' public-facing engagement contact. The story pulls together AZ pedestrian death data alongside Vision Zero gaps, and Phoenix Streets is the city most named in the data.

name: John Trierweiler
title: Senior Public Information Officer, Street Transportation
hook: Senior PIO for the agency that runs the Phoenix Road Safety Action Plan. Investigation gives a third-party data view of where that plan's miles, designs, and crashes intersect.
personalization_hook: Saw your March 2026 Phoenix Streets communications on the city's road-safety work as the senior PIO speaking for the department.

name: Lauren Evans
title: Public Information Officer, Street Transportation
hook: Phoenix Streets PIO. The investigation will keep moving in local media and the department needs a heads-up on the data framing before it lands in azcentral or AZ Family next.

name: Erik Guderian
title: Deputy Transportation Director
hook: Mesa's deputy transportation director. The investigation's geometric call-outs (intersection design, signal timing, crosswalk placement) are exactly his deputy-level scope.
personalization_hook: Saw your December 2, 2025 promotion to Mesa Transportation Director after serving as Interim since RJ Zeder's passing.

name: Sabine King
title: City Traffic Engineer
hook: Mesa's city traffic engineer signs off on signal timing and lane geometry. Investigation calls out engineered road choices as upstream causes of AZ pedestrian deaths, and the traffic engineer is exactly who owns that lever.
personalization_hook: Saw your Transportation Advisory Board presentations on speed cushion installations and Mesa traffic safety measures.

name: Beth Huning
title: City Engineer
hook: Mesa City Engineer owns capital project specs. Investigation's engineering frame puts capital project standards squarely in scope.
personalization_hook: Saw your APWA Top Ten Public Works Leader of the Year recognition and your work leading Mesa's capital infrastructure portfolio.

name: Erin Walsh
title: PIO, Transportation & Infrastructure
hook: Scottsdale's transportation comms lead. The Scottsdale corridor (Scottsdale Road, Shea, Pima) shows up in any AZ pedestrian death dataset. Walsh briefs Scottsdale press on every transportation story that hits.
personalization_hook: Saw your Pima Road expansion communications keeping Scottsdale residents looped in on the construction running through 2025 and 2026.

name: Cristina Lenko
title: PIO, Transportation & Infrastructure (part-time)
hook: Scottsdale Transportation backup PIO. Investigation maps fatalities to engineered road decisions, Scottsdale arterials show up in that data, Lenko handles overflow when Walsh is out.

name: Mesa Transportation Department
title: Department Inbox
hook: Department inbox routed through Mesa's senior transportation staff. Investigation tags Mesa specifically in the East Valley pedestrian data.
personalization_hook: Saw your recommendation to abandon US Route 60 right of way to the City of Mesa.

name: Glendale Transportation Services
title: Department Inbox
hook: Glendale Transportation's only published department contact. The West Valley shows up in AZ pedestrian death rate data and Glendale doesn't publish individual staff emails.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Pedestrian deaths in Arizona nearly double national rate" piece in Red Rock News on December 12, 2025.

name: Chandler Transit Division
title: Division Inbox
hook: Chandler Transportation Policy Division's published contact. Investigation pulls AZ-wide pedestrian fatality patterns, Chandler arterials are part of that picture, and this is the routing for any policy-side reply.

name: Phoenix Road Safety Department
title: Road Safety Action Plan Inbox
hook: Phoenix Road Safety Action Plan's direct inbox. Investigation gives an outside-data view of where that plan's spend isn't moving the fatality curve.

name: Phoenix Street Transportation Department
title: Department Inbox
hook: Phoenix Streets primary department contact. Investigation reframes AZ pedestrian deaths as a road-design issue and Phoenix Streets is the agency named in any AZ-wide road-design data.

name: Mara White
title: Outreach, Coalition of Arizona Bicyclists
hook: CAzBike's outreach contact. Investigation is the kind of statewide data piece their advocacy uses to push for design changes at MAG and ADOT.

name: EJ Montini
title: News Columnist
hook: Long-running AZ Republic columnist. Pedestrian death data with a road-design frame fits his column's policy-failure beat.
personalization_hook: Saw your May 19, 2025 KJZZ segment with Elvia Diaz on AI-generated testimony having no place in Arizona courtrooms.

name: Mark Carlisle
title: Associate Editor
hook: Already on the Phoenix pedestrian crossing beat. His 2023 piece on Phoenix exploring safer crossings overlaps directly with the AZ Law Now investigation's frame.
personalization_hook: Saw your Daily Independent reporting on the Moreland affordable housing groundbreaking and the Bell Road Denny's-to-McDonald's swap.

name: AZ Opinions desk
title: Opinion editor inbox
hook: Independent Newsmedia AZ opinion intake. Investigation runs as op-ed material for community papers across the West and East Valley.

name: Dianna M. Nanez
story_relevance_note: Investigative editor with indigenous/borderlands focus. Top routing target for ICE detention lawsuit, school restraint data (tribal-school overlap), pedestrian deaths on tribal land, Walgreens opioid (Navajo/Hopi corridor stores).
personalization_hook: Saw your “A Border Tribe, and the Wall that will Divide It” in USA Today on the Tigua tribe's border wall opposition.

name: Chelsea Curtis
story_relevance_note: MMIP/tribal beat. Match for stories with tribal-jurisdiction angles: AZ 325 educator discipline (BIE schools), school restraint (tribal schools), pedestrian deaths on Navajo/Hopi reservation roads, Walgreens opioid stores in border-tribal corridor.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Too long without answers: Shining a light on missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls, two-spirit and transgender people" in AZ Luminaria on the MMIWG2T crisis.

name: Itzia Crespo
story_relevance_note: Social. Useful when investigation has shareable visuals or video (pedestrian deaths road redesign, IAQ filter bypass schematics).

name: Hava Herzog
story_relevance_note: Police/fire/public safety beat. Top match for AZ Mayes ICE detention lawsuit (law enforcement angle), grand court Mesa HB2228 (elder abuse criminal investigation), pedestrian deaths road design (PD crash data).

name: Warren Adams-Ockrassa
story_relevance_note: White Mountain region editor. Match for Navajo/Apache county investigations: AZ 325 educator discipline (rural districts), school restraint data, pedestrian deaths (rural road design), opioid Walgreens stores in Show Low/Pinetop, IAQ MERV-13 in mountain schools.

name: Alexis Bechman
story_relevance_note: Gila County / Rim Country editor. Match for Gila-county angles: educator discipline (Payson Unified), opioid Walgreens (Payson store), pedestrian/road safety on SR-87.

name: Peter Aleshire
story_relevance_note: Veteran AZ reporter (longtime AZ journalism). Strong byline. Match for environmental/IAQ schools angle, road safety, opioid harm-reduction stories.
personalization_hook: Saw your article on the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation's environmental impact report for critical water shortages in the two giant reservoirs.

name: Tony Tedford
story_relevance_note: Multimedia producer. Useful for video-friendly stories: pedestrian deaths at named crash sites, school bus safety, IAQ filter swap-out demos.

name: Romalita Laban
story_relevance_note: Hopi Tribe official newspaper editor; would route any AZ stories impacting Hopi members (IAQ schools on rez, ICE detention of tribal members, road safety on US-264).

name: Jami Jones
story_relevance_note: OOIDA Land Line EIC; would route AZ pedestrian-deaths road-design story (truck-route safety angle) and Buckeye Durango/Yuma roundabout rejection (freight corridor design).

name: Mark Schremmer
story_relevance_note: Land Line senior editor; covers federal regulation impacting truckers, would route AZ road safety / pedestrian death stories.
personalization_hook: Saw your "FMCSA moves forward with regulatory purge" piece in Land Line Media on Feb. 19.

name: Keith Goble
story_relevance_note: Land Line state legislative editor; tracks AZ trucking/road-design legislation, primary route for Buckeye roundabout-rejection and AZ road safety stories.
personalization_hook: Saw your coverage of Arizona's two new truck route laws, SB1098 on signage uniformity and SB1097 on the restriction process, in Land Line Media.

name: Tyson Fisher
story_relevance_note: Land Line associate editor; route AZ pedestrian death road design and roundabout safety stories from a freight perspective.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Congressional crosshairs" piece in the February 2024 Land Line Magazine on New York City truck routes overhaul.

name: Damien Newton
story_relevance_note: Streetsblog publisher across CA; would route AZ pedestrian-deaths road-design story (Sun Belt comparison angle) and Tempe ASU pavement story.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Bad Road Design Is Stoking the U.S. Pedestrian Death Crisis" on Streetsblog California linking Sun Belt arterials to rising fatalities.

name: Joe Linton
story_relevance_note: Streetsblog LA editor; route AZ pedestrian fatality road-design and Buckeye roundabout-rejection stories.

name: Sahra Sulaiman
story_relevance_note: Streetsblog LA communities editor; equity angle on AZ pedestrian deaths and Tempe ASU pavement claim, especially in lower-income neighborhoods.
personalization_hook: Saw your "With the City Failing on Vision Zero, Streetsblog Looks at One of the Lead Implementing Agencies: LAPD" piece on Streetsblog LA in 2024.

name: Doreen Garlid
story_relevance_note: Tempe Vice Mayor; primary route for Tempe ASU pavement 180-day claim-clock story and pedestrian-death road-design stories in Tempe.

name: Christine Grundy
story_relevance_note: Mayor Orsborn's gatekeeper aide (only public name+email pairing on Buckeye site); route the Buckeye Durango/Yuma roundabout-rejection story.
